data.content:
### Protection Bypass Header

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

Configure the x-vercel-protection-bypass header to bypass deployment protection. This is the recommended method for automated tooling to authenticate requests to protected deployments.

```APIDOC
## Protection Bypass Request Header

### Description
Bypass Vercel Deployment Protection by setting the x-vercel-protection-bypass header with a generated secret. This header allows automated tooling to access protected deployments.

### Header Parameter
**x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The generated secret for the project that bypasses deployment protection

### Alternative: Query Parameter
If headers cannot be set, use as query parameter: `?x-vercel-protection-bypass=your-generated-secret`

### Request Example
```
GET /your-deployment.vercel.app HTTP/1.1
Host: your-deployment.vercel.app
x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
The request bypasses deployment protection and returns the protected content.

### Notes
- The secret is automatically added to deployments as the `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` environment variable
- The secret is set when a deployment is built
- Regenerating the secret invalidates previous deployments
- Redeploy your application after updating the secret to use the new value
```

--------------------------------

### PATCH /v1/projects/{projectId}/protection-bypass

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Updates the deployment protection automation bypass settings for a specified project. This endpoint allows modification of bypass rules that control which automated deployments can bypass protection measures. Requires appropriate permissions and may require a plan upgrade for advanced protection features.

```APIDOC
## PATCH /v1/projects/{projectId}/protection-bypass

### Description
Update the deployment protection automation bypass for a project. This allows configuration of bypass rules for automated deployments while maintaining security controls.

### Method
PATCH

### Endpoint
/v1/projects/{projectId}/protection-bypass

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **projectId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the project

#### Request Body
- **bypassRules** (array) - Optional - Array of automation bypass rules
- **enabled** (boolean) - Optional - Enable or disable protection bypass for automation

### Response Codes
#### Success Response (200)
- **protectionBypass** (object) - Updated protection bypass configuration
- **enabled** (boolean) - Current status of automation bypass
- **bypassRules** (array) - List of configured bypass rules

#### Error Responses
- **400** - Invalid request parameters or malformed request body
- **403** - You do not have permission to access this resource
- **404** - Project not found
- **409** - The project is currently being transferred or name conflict
- **428** - Owner does not have protection add-on; Advanced Deployment Protection is not available for the user plan

### Notes
- Deploying to Serverless Functions to multiple regions requires a plan update
- Advanced Deployment Protection requires appropriate plan and add-ons
- Owner must have protection add-on enabled for this feature
```

--------------------------------

### Set HTTP Header for Vercel Protection Bypass Secret

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

To bypass Vercel Deployment Protection, include the `x-vercel-protection-bypass` HTTP header or query parameter in your requests. Its value must be the project's generated automation bypass secret. This method is crucial for automated tooling to access protected deployments.

```http
x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret (required)
```

--------------------------------

### Bypass Deployment Protection

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

This mechanism allows bypassing Vercel Deployment Protection for automated tooling by including a specific header or query parameter in requests to your deployment.

```APIDOC
## ANY /api/your-deployment-path

### Description
This mechanism allows bypassing Vercel Deployment Protection for automated tooling by including a specific header or query parameter in requests to your deployment.

### Method
ANY

### Endpoint
/[your-deployment-path]

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The generated secret for the project.

#### Request Headers
- **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The generated secret for the project.

### Request Example
{}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Note** - The request will bypass deployment protection and proceed to the intended resource.

#### Response Example
{}
```

--------------------------------

### PATCH /v1/projects/{idOrName}/protection-bypass

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/update-protection-bypass-for-automation

This endpoint allows you to update the deployment protection automation bypass for a specific project. You can revoke existing bypasses, regenerate new ones, or generate a new random bypass.

```APIDOC
## PATCH /v1/projects/{idOrName}/protection-bypass

### Description
Update the deployment protection automation bypass for a project.

### Method
PATCH

### Endpoint
/v1/projects/{idOrName}/protection-bypass

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **idOrName** (string) - Required - The unique project identifier or the project name

#### Query Parameters
- **teamId** (string) - Optional - The Team identifier to perform the request on behalf of.
- **slug** (string) - Optional - The Team slug to perform the request on behalf of.

#### Request Body
- **revoke** (object) - Optional - Optional instructions for revoking and regenerating an automation bypass
  - **secret** (string) - Required - Automation bypass to revoked
  - **regenerate** (boolean) - Required - Whether or not a new automation bypass should be created after the provided secret is revoked
- **generate** (object) - Optional - Generate a new secret. If neither generate nor revoke are provided, a new random secret will be generated.
  - **secret** (string) - Optional - Optional value of the secret to generate (pattern: ^[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$)

### Request Example
```json
{
  "generate": {
    "secret": "mycustomsecret1234567890123456789012"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **protectionBypass** (object) - A map of protection bypass configurations. Keys are arbitrary strings, and values are objects representing a bypass.
  - Example Bypass Object (type 1):
    - **createdAt** (number) - Creation timestamp.
    - **createdBy** (string) - ID of the user who created the bypass.
    - **scope** (string, enum: "integration-automation-bypass") - Scope of the bypass.
    - **integrationId** (string) - ID of the integration.
    - **configurationId** (string) - ID of the configuration.
  - Example Bypass Object (type 2):
    - **createdAt** (number) - Creation timestamp.
    - **createdBy** (string) - ID of the user who created the bypass.
    - **scope** (string, enum: "automation-bypass") - Scope of the bypass.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "protectionBypass": {
    "automationBypassKey1": {
      "createdAt": 1678886400000,
      "createdBy": "usr_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
      "scope": "automation-bypass"
    },
    "integrationBypassKey2": {
      "createdAt": 1678886400000,
      "createdBy": "usr_yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy",
      "scope": "integration-automation-bypass",
      "integrationId": "int_abcdefg",
      "configurationId": "conf_hijklmn"
    }
  }
}
```

#### Error Response (400)
(No specific schema provided)
```

--------------------------------

### Deployment Protection Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Configure password protection and security settings for deployments. Supports different deployment types (all, preview, production) with optional password protection and automation bypass headers.

```APIDOC
## Deployment Protection Configuration

### Description
Configure password protection and security settings for deployments across different deployment targets.

### Properties

#### deploymentType
- **Type**: string (enum)
- **Required**: Yes
- **Description**: Specify which deployments the protection applies to
- **Allowed Values**:
  - `all` - All deployments
  - `preview` - Preview deployments only
  - `prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews` - Production URLs and all previews
  - `all_except_custom_domains` - All except custom domains

#### passwordProtect
- **Type**: object
- **Required**: No
- **Nullable**: Yes
- **Description**: Password protection settings for deployments
- **Sub-properties**:
  - **deploymentType** (string, enum) - Required. Target deployment type (all, preview, production, prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews, all_except_custom_domains)

#### automationBypassSecret
- **Type**: object
- **Required**: No
- **Nullable**: Yes
- **Description**: Automation bypass secret for bypassing deployment protection
- **Sub-properties**:
  - **deploymentType** (string, enum) - Required. Target deployment type

### Request Example
```json
{
  "deploymentType": "all",
  "passwordProtect": {
    "deploymentType": "preview"
  },
  "automationBypassSecret": {
    "deploymentType": "all"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Update Protection Bypass for URL

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Update the protection bypass settings for a deployment alias or URL. This endpoint manages user access, comment access, and shareable links for deployments and preview comments. Returns the updated list of aliases with their protection bypass configurations.

```APIDOC
## PATCH /v12/deployments/{deploymentId}/aliases/{aliasId}/protection-bypass

### Description
Update the protection bypass for an alias or deployment URL. This controls user access, comment access for deployments, and shareable links used for Vercel Authentication and Preview Comments.

### Method
PATCH

### Endpoint
/v12/deployments/{deploymentId}/aliases/{aliasId}/protection-bypass

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **deploymentId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the deployment
- **aliasId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the alias

#### Request Body
- **access** (string) - Optional - Access level: "requested" or "granted"
- **scope** (string) - Optional - Scope of the protection bypass (e.g., "user", "alias-protection-override", "email_invite")
- **createdAt** (number) - Optional - Timestamp when the bypass was created
- **lastUpdatedAt** (number) - Optional - Timestamp of last update
- **lastUpdatedBy** (string) - Optional - User ID of the last updater
- **createdBy** (string) - Optional - User ID of the creator

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
Returns the updated list of aliases with their protection bypass configurations.

- **aliases** (array) - List of aliases assigned to the deployment
  - **uid** (string) - Unique identifier for the alias
  - **alias** (string) - The alias domain (e.g., "my-alias.vercel.app")
  - **created** (string) - ISO timestamp when the alias was created
  - **redirect** (string) - Optional redirect URL
  - **protectionBypass** (object) - Protection bypass configuration with access and scope settings

#### Response Example
{
  "aliases": [
    {
      "uid": "2WjyKQmM8ZnGcJsPWMrHRHrE",
      "alias": "my-alias.vercel.app",
      "created": "2017-04-26T23:00:34.232Z",
      "redirect": null,
      "protectionBypass": {
        "createdAt": 1234567890,
        "lastUpdatedAt": 1234567890,
        "lastUpdatedBy": "user-id",
        "access": "granted",
        "scope": "user"
      }
    }
  ]
}

### Error Responses
#### 400 Bad Request
One of the provided values in the request query is invalid.

#### 401 Unauthorized
The request is not authorized.

#### 403 Forbidden
You do not have permission to access this resource.

#### 404 Not Found
The deployment was not found.
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright Configuration Example

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

Example configuration for using Protection Bypass for Automation with Playwright E2E testing framework. Shows how to set the bypass header in Playwright configuration.

```APIDOC
## Playwright E2E Testing Configuration

### Description
Configure Playwright to use Protection Bypass for Automation by setting extra HTTP headers with the generated secret.

### Configuration File
**playwright.config.ts**

### Configuration Example
```typescript
import { PlaywrightTestConfig } from '@playwright/test';

const config: PlaywrightTestConfig = {
  use: {
    extraHTTPHeaders: {
      'x-vercel-protection-bypass': process.env.VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET,
      'x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie': true | 'samesitenone'
    }
  }
}

export default config;
```

### Environment Variable
**VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET** - Automatically set on deployments. Contains the generated secret for bypassing protection.

### Implementation Steps
1. Generate a protection bypass secret in project settings
2. The secret is automatically added as `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` environment variable
3. Configure Playwright with the environment variable in extraHTTPHeaders
4. Set `x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie` to `true` for in-browser testing or `samesitenone` for iframe access
5. Run Playwright tests against protected deployments

### Notes
- Redeploy application after regenerating the secret to use new value
- Available to team members with member role or project administrators
```

--------------------------------

### Protection Bypass Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/create-a-new-project

Configure protection bypass rules for Vercel projects. This allows defining security exceptions through integration automation or manual automation bypasses with specific scope and creator tracking.

```APIDOC
## Protection Bypass Rules

### Description
Manages security protection bypass configurations for integration automation and manual automation scenarios.

### Configuration Schema

#### Integration Automation Bypass
- **createdAt** (number) - Required - Unix timestamp when bypass was created
- **createdBy** (string) - Required - User or service that created the bypass
- **scope** (string) - Required - Enum: `integration-automation-bypass` - Scope of the bypass
- **integrationId** (string) - Required - Associated integration identifier
- **configurationId** (string) - Required - Associated configuration identifier

#### Automation Bypass
- **createdAt** (number) - Required - Unix timestamp when bypass was created
- **createdBy** (string) - Required - User or service that created the bypass
- **scope** (string) - Required - Enum: `automation-bypass` - Scope of the bypass

### Request Example - Integration Automation
```json
{
  "createdAt": 1704067200,
  "createdBy": "user@example.com",
  "scope": "integration-automation-bypass",
  "integrationId": "int_123456",
  "configurationId": "config_789"
}
```

### Request Example - Automation
```json
{
  "createdAt": 1704067200,
  "createdBy": "automation-service",
  "scope": "automation-bypass"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Alias Protection Bypass Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/aliases/get-an-alias

Defines the protection bypass configuration for aliases, including creation metadata and scope information. The protection bypass allows controlled access to aliased deployments with tracking of who created or modified the bypass and when.

```APIDOC
## Alias Protection Bypass Object

### Description
Defines protection bypass settings for an alias, including creation and modification metadata.

### Properties

#### createdAt
- **Type:** number
- **Required:** Yes
- **Description:** Unix timestamp indicating when the protection bypass was created

#### lastUpdatedAt
- **Type:** number
- **Required:** Yes
- **Description:** Unix timestamp indicating when the protection bypass was last modified

#### lastUpdatedBy
- **Type:** string
- **Required:** Yes
- **Description:** User identifier or email of the person who last updated the protection bypass

#### scope
- **Type:** string (enum)
- **Required:** Yes
- **Allowed Values:** `email_invite`
- **Description:** The scope of the protection bypass, defining the type of bypass mechanism

### Response Example
```json
{
  "createdAt": 1672531200,
  "lastUpdatedAt": 1672617600,
  "lastUpdatedBy": "user@example.com",
  "scope": "email_invite"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Set Bypass Cookie Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

Configure the x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie header to set authorization bypass as a cookie for follow-up requests. Useful for in-browser testing and non-direct access scenarios.

```APIDOC
## Set Bypass Cookie Header

### Description
Set authorization bypass as a cookie using a redirect with Set-Cookie header for follow-up requests in automated testing scenarios.

### Header Parameter
**x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie** (string) - Optional - Set to `true` or `samesitenone`

### Values
- **true** - Sets bypass cookie with `SameSite=Lax` (default)
- **samesitenone** - Sets bypass cookie with `SameSite=None` for non-direct access (e.g., iframe)

### Request Example
```
GET /your-deployment.vercel.app HTTP/1.1
Host: your-deployment.vercel.app
x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret
x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: true
```

### Response
#### Success Response (302)
Returns a redirect with Set-Cookie header containing the bypass authorization.

```
HTTP/1.1 302 Found
Set-Cookie: [bypass-cookie]; SameSite=Lax; Path=/
Location: /your-deployment.vercel.app
```

### Use Cases
- In-browser E2E testing with follow-up requests
- Accessing deployment within iframe (use `samesitenone`)
- Maintaining bypass authorization across multiple requests
```

--------------------------------

### Set HTTP Header for Vercel Bypass Cookie with SameSite=None

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

If accessing deployments non-directly, such as within an iframe, configure `x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie` with `samesitenone`. This sets the `SameSite` attribute of the bypass cookie to `None`. It ensures the cookie is properly handled across different sites.

```http
x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: samesitenone (optional)
```

--------------------------------

### PUT /websites/{id}/protection-bypass - Update Protection Bypass Rules

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Updates protection bypass rules for a website. Supports both integration automation bypass and standard automation bypass scopes. Rules track creation metadata and can be scoped to specific integrations and configurations.

```APIDOC
## PUT /websites/{id}/protection-bypass

### Description
Updates protection bypass rules to allow specified integrations or automation to bypass protection checks. Supports multiple bypass scopes with creation tracking.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
/websites/{id}/protection-bypass

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **id** (string) - Required - The website identifier

#### Request Body
- **scope** (string) - Required - Bypass scope: 'integration-automation-bypass' or 'automation-bypass'
- **createdAt** (number) - Required - Unix timestamp of rule creation
- **createdBy** (string) - Required - User or system identifier who created the rule
- **integrationId** (string) - Optional - Integration identifier (required for 'integration-automation-bypass' scope)
- **configurationId** (string) - Optional - Configuration identifier (required for 'integration-automation-bypass' scope)

### Request Example
{
  "integration-automation-bypass": {
    "scope": "integration-automation-bypass",
    "createdAt": 1234567890,
    "createdBy": "user@example.com",
    "integrationId": "int_abc123",
    "configurationId": "cfg_xyz789"
  },
  "automation-bypass": {
    "scope": "automation-bypass",
    "createdAt": 1234567891,
    "createdBy": "system"
  }
}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **scope** (string) - The bypass scope type
- **createdAt** (number) - Creation timestamp
- **createdBy** (string) - Creator identifier
- **integrationId** (string) - Integration identifier (if applicable)
- **configurationId** (string) - Configuration identifier (if applicable)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright `extraHTTPHeaders` for Vercel Protection Bypass

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/security/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

Integrate Vercel protection bypass into Playwright tests by setting `extraHTTPHeaders` in `playwright.config.ts`. Include `x-vercel-protection-bypass` with `process.env.VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET`. Optionally, add `x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie` to manage bypass cookies for multi-request flows.

```typescript
const config: PlaywrightTestConfig = {
  use: {
    extraHTTPHeaders: {
      'x-vercel-protection-bypass': process.env.VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET,
      'x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie': true | 'samesitenone' (optional)
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Protection Bypass Configuration Schema

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/update-the-data-cache-feature

Defines protection bypass configurations that allow bypassing protection mechanisms for specific integrations and automation scenarios. Tracks who created the bypass and when, with scope and integration identifiers.

```APIDOC
## Protection Bypass Configuration

### Description
Manages protection bypass settings for integrations and automation, allowing specific configurations to bypass protection mechanisms with audit trail information.

### Schema Properties

#### Protection Bypass Object
- **protectionBypass** (object with additional properties) - Collection of bypass configurations

#### Bypass Configuration Properties
- **createdAt** (number) - Required. Unix timestamp when the bypass was created
- **createdBy** (string) - Required. User or system identifier that created the bypass
- **scope** (string) - Required. Scope of the bypass. Enum: integration-automation-bypass
- **integrationId** (string) - Required. Identifier of the integration using this bypass
- **configurationId** (string) - Required. Configuration identifier associated with the bypass

### Response Example
{
  "protectionBypass": {
    "bypass_key_1": {
      "createdAt": 1234567890,
      "createdBy": "user_123",
      "scope": "integration-automation-bypass",
      "integrationId": "int_abc123",
      "configurationId": "cfg_xyz789"
    }
  }
}
```
